findings.description: clamp_to_range does not validate tz-awareness, so mixing naive and aware datetimes raises an unguarded TypeError on comparison, unlike humanize_delta which checks explicitly.
start_of_day annotates the parameter as date but silently accepts a datetime subclass, discarding its time component and existing tzinfo without warning.
humanize_delta's coarsest bucket is weeks, so deltas of months or years render as unbounded 'N w ago' (e.g. '520 w ago'), arguably violating the 'coarse' intent.
strptime with %Y accepts variable-width/low years (e.g. '1-1-1' parses to year 1), so parsing is less strict than the YYYY-MM-DD docstring implies.
int() truncation of total_seconds() toward zero maps a sub-second future moment to 0, returning 'just now' instead of 'in the future'.
day_range materializes the entire date list, so very large spans (years/decades) allocate O(n) memory where a generator could suffice.
business_days_between builds the full day_range list before summing, doubling transient memory for large ranges instead of streaming.
tzinfo-presence check does not guarantee a usable offset; a tzinfo whose utcoffset() returns None would pass the guard yet still be effectively naive.
is_weekend is the only public function lacking a docstring, inconsistent with the module's documentation style.
divmod reuses the name 'seconds' from the outer scope, shadowing it and slightly reducing readability.
Module defines no __all__, leaving the public export surface implicit for a shared utility module.
